prompt:
While researching a museum lighting upgrade, a student took the following notes:
• New LED lights replaced old halogen bulbs.
• The lights give off far less heat.
• Fragile paintings now fade more slowly.

The student wants to emphasize the benefit to the artwork. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Compared with the bulbs they replaced, the new lights installed throughout the galleries produce noticeably less heat.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the benefit to the artwork as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A museum, a place that collects and displays objects of interest, must balance public access with careful conservation.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the benefit to the artwork as the prompt asks.

choices.text: As part of the upgrade, the museum swapped out all of its old halogen bulbs for modern, efficient LED lights.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the benefit to the artwork as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Because the new LED lights give off far less heat than the old halogen bulbs, the museum's fragile paintings now fade more slowly.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize the benefit to the artwork.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the benefit to the artwork; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a library renovation, a student took the following notes:
• The library added a quiet study wing.
• Weekly visits rose by a third.
• Evening hours were extended.

The student wants to report the effect of the changes. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: A public library, which lends books and other materials at no charge, serves readers of every age in its community.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the effect of the changes as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Weekly visits to the library rose by a full third, a jump the staff were pleased to see after months of construction.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the effect of the changes as the prompt asks.

choices.text: After the library added a quiet study wing and extended evening hours, weekly visits rose by a third.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to report the effect of the changes.

choices.text: As part of the renovation, the library added a brand-new quiet study wing and kept its doors open later into the evening.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not report the effect of the changes as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to report the effect of the changes; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching an electric bus rollout, a student took the following notes:
• The agency replaced 50 diesel buses.
• The fleet now runs nearly silent.
• Riders report a smoother, cleaner ride.

The student wants to describe how the change affected riders. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Now that 50 diesel buses have been replaced, riders enjoy a fleet that runs nearly silent and gives a smoother, cleaner ride.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to describe how the change affected riders.

choices.text: The new electric buses move so quietly that pedestrians sometimes fail to hear one approaching the curb.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe how the change affected riders as the prompt asks.

choices.text: An electric bus, powered by rechargeable batteries instead of fuel, produces no exhaust as it travels its route.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe how the change affected riders as the prompt asks.

choices.text: In a major shift, the transit agency went ahead and replaced fifty of its aging diesel buses with new models.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not describe how the change affected riders as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to describe how the change affected riders; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a wind turbine farm, a student took the following notes:
• The farm has 40 turbines on a ridge.
• Each turbine stands 120 meters tall.
• Together they power 30,000 homes.

The student wants to convey the farm's overall scale. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: The forty turbines were installed along a windswept ridge chosen for its steady and reliable seasonal breezes.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the farm's overall scale as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A wind turbine, which turns moving air into electricity, has become a familiar sight across many rural landscapes.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the farm's overall scale as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Standing on the ridge, each of the farm's turbines reaches an impressive height of about one hundred twenty meters.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the farm's overall scale as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Forty turbines, each rising 120 meters along the ridge, together generate enough electricity to power 30,000 homes.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to convey the farm's overall scale.

explanation: The goal is to convey the farm's overall scale; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a language preservation effort, a student took the following notes:
• Only 200 fluent speakers of the language remain.
• A school began teaching it to children in 2020.
• Enrollment has grown each year since.

The student wants to convey both the threat and the response. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: A language, which is a shared system that lets a community communicate, can fade once its fluent speakers grow few.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey both the threat and the response as the prompt asks.

choices.text: In 2020 a school began teaching the language to children, and the number enrolling in those classes has grown each year.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey both the threat and the response as the prompt asks.

choices.text: With only 200 fluent speakers left, a school began teaching the language to children in 2020, and enrollment has grown every year since.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to convey both the threat and the response.

choices.text: With only about 200 fluent speakers still living, the language is in real danger of disappearing within a generation.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey both the threat and the response as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to convey both the threat and the response; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a historic theater restoration, a student took the following notes:
• The theater opened in 1912.
• A fire damaged it badly in 1980.
• Donors funded a full restoration this year.

The student wants to present the building's troubled history. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: A theater, a building made for staging plays and concerts, often serves as a gathering place for a whole community.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the building's troubled history as the prompt asks.

choices.text: When the theater first opened its doors to audiences back in 1912, it quickly became a centerpiece of the town.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the building's troubled history as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Opened in 1912, the theater was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1980 before donors finally funded a full restoration this year.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to present the building's troubled history.

choices.text: Thanks to a group of generous donors, the theater received a complete restoration during the course of this past year.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the building's troubled history as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to present the building's troubled history; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a recycling program, a student took the following notes:
• The town added curbside glass pickup.
• Glass recycling rose 60% in one year.
• Landfill waste dropped noticeably.

The student wants to emphasize the program's impact. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Glass recycling in the town rose by 60% over a single year, a figure that the program's organizers found encouraging.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the program's impact as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The town decided to add curbside glass pickup so that residents would no longer need to haul their bottles to a center.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the program's impact as the prompt asks.

choices.text: After the town added curbside glass pickup, glass recycling jumped 60% and landfill waste dropped noticeably.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize the program's impact.

choices.text: Recycling, the process of collecting used materials so they can be remade, is practiced by many towns in different ways.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the program's impact as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the program's impact; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a peat bog excavation, a student took the following notes:
• The bog preserved a 4,000-year-old canoe.
• Low oxygen kept the wood from rotting.
• The find reveals ancient travel routes.

The student wants to explain why the canoe survived so long. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Buried deep in the peat, the canoe turned out to be roughly four thousand years old when experts examined it.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why the canoe survived so long as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The well-preserved canoe pulled from the bog offers fresh clues about the routes ancient people once traveled.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why the canoe survived so long as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A bog, a soft wetland built up from layers of decaying plants, forms slowly over many thousands of years.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why the canoe survived so long as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Because the bog's waterlogged, low-oxygen mud kept the wood from rotting, a canoe survived there for some 4,000 years.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain why the canoe survived so long.

explanation: The goal is to explain why the canoe survived so long; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a deep-sea expedition, a student took the following notes:
• A submersible reached 6,000 meters down.
• It filmed creatures never seen before.
• Crushing pressure required a titanium hull.

The student wants to emphasize the difficulty of the conditions. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: A submersible, a small craft designed for underwater travel, can carry researchers far deeper than ordinary divers go.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the difficulty of the conditions as the prompt asks.

choices.text: During the dive, the expedition's cameras captured footage of several deep-sea creatures never before seen by anyone.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the difficulty of the conditions as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The submersible used on the expedition was built with a strong titanium hull to keep its crew safe below the surface.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the difficulty of the conditions as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Withstanding crushing pressure that demanded a titanium hull, the submersible descended 6,000 meters to film creatures never seen before.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize the difficulty of the conditions.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the difficulty of the conditions; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a freshwater turtle study, a student took the following notes:
• Females nest on the same beach for life.
• Warmer sand produces more female hatchlings.
• Researchers tagged 200 turtles over five years.

The student wants to highlight the link between temperature and sex. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: A turtle, a reptile protected by a hard shell, can live for many decades in the right freshwater habitat.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the link between temperature and sex as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Over the course of five years, the patient researchers managed to tag and track a total of two hundred turtles.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the link between temperature and sex as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The female turtles in the study return to nest on the very same stretch of beach throughout their entire lives.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the link between temperature and sex as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Because warmer sand produces more female hatchlings, the temperature of a nesting beach can tip a turtle population toward females.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to highlight the link between temperature and sex.

explanation: The goal is to highlight the link between temperature and sex; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a public seed library, a student took the following notes:
• Gardeners borrow seeds for free.
• They return seeds saved from their harvest.
• The collection favors local heirloom varieties.

The student wants to explain how the library sustains itself. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: A seed, the small package from which a plant grows, can stay viable for years if it is stored properly.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the library sustains itself as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The seed library keeps going because gardeners who borrow seeds for free return new ones saved from their own harvests.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain how the library sustains itself.

choices.text: The collection housed at the seed library leans heavily toward heirloom varieties suited to the local climate.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the library sustains itself as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Any gardener in the community is welcome to borrow a packet of seeds from the library at no cost at all.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the library sustains itself as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain how the library sustains itself; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.
